g.text: Scene 1/5 — Inject rules at every prompt header
(UserPromptSubmit hook → ~490 token anchor)
$ echo {prompt} | karma_user_prompt_submit.py | jq additionalContext
[karma — long-term agreement anchor (compact form; full preferences injected at
session start)]
This turn: a quick name + one-line reminder of each direction. Full preference t
ext was injected at session start.
1. [long-term-fundamental] The user trusts you to dig into root causes. When fac
ing hard problems
2. [non-blocking-parallel] During sleep / wait / long-task execution, the user i
s waiting for your 〔Last response had drift on this one — let's realign this t
urn〕
3. [chinese-plain-no-jargon] The user you're collaborating with is non-technical
and only reads
4. [loud-failure-with-evidence
Scene 2/5 — Real-time block of sleep 30
(PreToolUse hook → non-blocking-parallel)
$ echo {tool=Bash, command=sleep 30} | karma_pre_tool_use.py
{"hookSpecificOutput": {"hookEventName": "PreToolUse", "permissionDecision": "de
ny", "permissionDecisionReason": "karma 拦截：违反 'non-blocking-parallel'。\n检
测到：Bash sleep command: 'sleep 30'\n方向：During sleep / wait / long-task exec
ution, the user is waiting for your\noutput. Staring at a progress bar isn't col
laboration — it's \"stuck.\"\nAfter kicking off a background task, immediately p
ush the next thing\nthat can be done (read related files / spawn another sub-age
nt / design\nnext step) — you'll be notified when the task completes. Multiple\n
independent tasks prefer parallel kick-off, don't serialize.\n建议：sleep period
s make the user wait; experience is 'stuck.' Switch to run_in_background=True ki
cking off the task, then immediately push the next doable thing (read related fi
les / spawn another sub-agent / design next step) — task completion will notify
you. For wait-on-condition, use Monitor + until loop."}}
Scene 3/5 — Catch short-term intent in Agent response
(Stop hook → long-term v0.11.0 response-level)
$ # Agent said: "Let me just hardcode this case to ship it"
$ echo {transcript_path} | karma_stop.py
Scene 4/5 — Nudge Agent to keep pushing after silent stop
(Stop hook → keep-pushing-no-stop)
$ # Agent said: "Task complete." (pure statement, no next step)
Scene 5/5 — Mid-conversation full reinject at decay threshold
(PostToolUse hook → byte_seq hits Opus 60K → full baseline)
$ # session state: tool_byte_seq=62000 (past Opus 60K threshold)
$ echo {tool=Read} | karma_post_tool_use.py | jq additionalContext (head)
[karma — Your long-term agreement with the user]
You're collaborating with a real human user who listed several long-term priorit
ies.
This isn't rules and isn't a judgment — these are the collaborative agreements t
hey hope to build with you.
1. The user trusts you to dig into root causes. When facing hard problems
they want you to pause and think "what's the cleanest solution?"
rather than "what's the fastest patch?" Short-term patches /
hardcoding / skip-verification flags are all "te...
That's karma in 60s — pure engineering, zero LLM.
github.com/jhaizhou-ops/karma
